name: Nigerian Pidgin
register: Reverent-but-warm contemporary Naija, matching the wording family of the Bible Society of Nigeria Pidgin Scriptures (the Nigerian Pidgin New Testament) as the reference tradition, tuned to the intimate address register Nigerian Pentecostal/charismatic believers actually use in prayer and worship. It should read as heart-speech to a beloved Papa, not the formal/legal register of English-language public life.
quote_convention: English-style curly double quotes for speech (“ ”), single quotes for nested speech (‘ ’); no guillemets.
poetry_convention: Poetry and psalm-lines set as broken lines with the second (and further) lines of each parallel couplet indented; verse numbering follows the English Bible.

glossary.t: our Papa wey dey for heaven
glossary.gloss: our Father who is in Heaven
glossary.note: Anchored to Nigerian Pidgin Bible (Mt 6:9) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Our Papa wey dey Heaven". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)

Anchored to Nigerian Pidgin Bible (Mt 6:9 / Rom 1:7) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Our Papa". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)

Anchored to Nigerian Pidgin Bible (Jn 14:9ff) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Papa". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)

Anchored to Nigerian Pidgin Bible (Mt 6:14) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Papa wey dey Heaven". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
Aramaic address retained + Naija gloss 'Papa'. Both are the intimate daddy-cry, so the pairing lands as one warm breath.
REVERSION TOKEN. Generic deity / God-as-God only; never a substitute for 'Papa'.

glossary.gloss: the Master / Boss — the covenant Lord (Yahweh)
glossary.note: REVERSION TOKEN, traditional non-filial covenant term (small-caps 'the LORD' may be kept per reference tradition — reviewer to confirm). 'Oga' as generic 'Lord/Master' title for Jesus is a distinct use. Never a substitute for 'Papa'.
Borrowed form is the settled term across Naija worship and the Pidgin Scriptures.

glossary.gloss: the Spirit belonging to our Father
glossary.note: Anchored to Nigerian Pidgin Bible (Mt 10:20) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "the Spirit of Our Papa". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
'Pikin' is the everyday native word for son/child; carries the messianic self-title without borrowing.
Jesus as the Father's own Pikin — deliberately mirrors 'Papa' so the family bond is heard.
Christological title. Reviewer to confirm vs 'Papa im Pikin' (the Father's Son).
'im' = his; 'only-one' = sole; the tender John 3:16 weight.

Anchored to Nigerian Pidgin Bible (Mt 26:29) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "Our Papa Kingdom". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
Standard form.
Settled titular form.
Transliterated; semantic twin of Christ, used where English keeps 'Messiah'.
Sacred-register term; everyday 'agriment' is avoided to keep the weight of a bound promise.
Renders Hebrew hesed (loyal covenant love); 'no dey change' voices the unwavering loyalty. Reviewer to confirm vs 'true-true love'.

glossary.t: tender mercy wey dey pain belle
glossary.gloss: tender mercy that aches in the belly (gut-deep pity)
glossary.note: Renders racham / splanchnizomai; the Naija idiom 'belle dey pain (person)' is the exact gut-level sense — in narrative use the verbal 'im belle dey pain am'.

glossary.gloss: being right and upright before God
glossary.note: Naija 'correct' = morally right/true; carries uprightness before Papa without a Latinate borrowing.
Plain durative negation; the promise of John 3:16.
Already a native Naija Christian idiom — no adaptation needed; John 3:3,7.
